title: Inventory training-provider, employment-outcome, labor-market, and skills-mismatch data sources for LMICs
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates training-provider registries, employment-outcome survey data, labor-force surveys, enterprise surveys, and skills-mismatch assessments into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit provider-data fragmentation and outcome-tracking documentation.
inputs: National training-provider registry documentation for target countries
Labor-force survey methodology and youth employment modules
Enterprise survey data on employer skill demands
ILO and World Bank skills-data documentation
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly provider-registry fragmentation and employment-outcome tracking rates.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering provider-data fragmentation, outcome-tracking rates, and informal-sector measurement gaps.

title: Map sub-national skills-training availability for at least two LMICs using provider registries and program documentation
outcome: A sub-national training-availability analysis for at least two LMICs, documenting provider-registry data sources, fragmentation across government/NGO/private sectors, and coverage gaps.
inputs: National training-provider registries for target countries
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with availability methodology and data-fragmentation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms provider-data extraction, fragmentation documentation, and coverage-gap assessment for each target country.
done_condition: Training-availability analysis includes methodology, provider-data fragmentation documentation, coverage gaps, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Analyze employment outcomes of skills-training programs using labor-force and enterprise survey data
outcome: An employment-outcome analysis linking training availability to labor-force survey employment data, with explicit documentation of outcome-tracking limitations, informal-sector measurement gaps, and the distinction between training enrollment and verified employment impact.
inputs: enterprise survey data on employer skill demands
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with outcome methodology and tracking-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms outcome-linkage methodology, informal-sector measurement documentation, and enrollment-vs-impact distinction.
done_condition: Employment-outcome analysis includes methodology, outcome-tracking limitations, informal-sector documentation, and replication status.

title: Red-team training-effectiveness overstatement and outcome-tracking-gap risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how training-availability maps could imply effectiveness without outcome data, how pooled effect estimates could mask program-design heterogeneity, and how informal-sector employment gaps could make formal outcome data misleading.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every effectiveness claim has outcome-tracking rate documentation, heterogeneity context, and informal-sector measurement caveats. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every training-effectiveness claim is either blocked, softened to outcome-data-limited language with explicit heterogeneity and informal-sector caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match training-provider and employer-organization decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the training-availability and employment-outcome analysis would change any real decision for training providers, employer organizations, or labor-ministry programs without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for training-provider and employer audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that availability maps imply effectiveness without outcome data, or that pooled effect estimates mask heterogeneity. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Kenya National Industrial Training Authority director), named decision (e.g., training-program expansion targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for program expansion without outcome evidence), and replicated field-reality review.
